pr.title: Match AObj's callbackForeachFunc and get rid of jump table in .data
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched sysdolphin baselib AObj callbackForeachFunc in C, replacing an inline asm implementation and eliminating its generated .data jump table jtbl_80405FEC from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.s. The change introduced a dedicated callbackArg union in aobj.h to model the callback argument as f32, u32, or void*, updated callbackForeachFunc to switch on AObj_Arg_Type and call the supplied callback with the correct argument shape, and adjusted one assert to use lbl_804D5D08 instead of __FILE__ for matching.
what_changed: Removed the .data jump table symbol jtbl_80405FEC from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.s; the table previously pointed to offsets inside callbackForeachFunc.
Replaced the inline asm body of callbackForeachFunc in src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.c with a C switch over AObj_Arg_Type.

Implemented callback dispatch cases for AOBJ_ARG_A, AOBJ_ARG_AF, AOBJ_ARG_AV, AOBJ_ARG_AU, AOBJ_ARG_AO, AOBJ_ARG_AOT, AOBJ_ARG_AOF, AOBJ_ARG_AOV, AOBJ_ARG_AOU, AOBJ_ARG_AOTF, AOBJ_ARG_AOTV, and AOBJ_ARG_AOTU.
Added typedef union _callbackArg in aobj.h with f32 f, u32 d, and void* v fields.
Changed callbackForeachFunc's final argument type from FObjData* to callbackArg* in both the definition and declaration.
Moved #pragma pop to after the new C callbackForeachFunc body.
Changed HSD_AObjAlloc's failed-allocation assert file argument from __FILE__ to lbl_804D5D08, likely to preserve string/reference matching.
smart_moves: Modeled the callback payload as a small union rather than reusing FObjData, making the C signature match the observed per-case usage: float, integer data, or pointer.
Used an explicit switch on AObj_Arg_Type to reproduce the former jump-table dispatch while allowing the compiler to own the control flow and remove the hand-authored asm.
Kept each switch case as a direct callback call followed by return, mirroring the old assembly's branch-to-exit structure.
Handled AOBJ_ARG_AOTU with an explicit (u32)arg->d cast, preserving the integer argument intent at the call site.
Removed the obsolete external jump table once the function matched in C, reducing leftover .data assembly clutter.
decomp_lessons: When an inline asm function is primarily a type-dependent dispatcher, matching can often come from reconstructing the enum-controlled switch and using the right callback argument container.
A jump table in .data may disappear naturally once the matched C switch is compiled, but only if the case ordering and argument-passing patterns line up with the original code.
Callback-heavy code may require intentionally loose function pointer typing, such as void (*func)(), because each enum variant supplies a different apparent callback signature.
If an existing data type name is misleading for a callback argument, adding a narrow union can improve both matching and readability without inventing a larger struct.
String identity can matter for matching: replacing __FILE__ with an existing label such as lbl_804D5D08 may be necessary when the expected binary references a specific string symbol.
naming_conventions: AObj argument-format enum values use the AOBJ_ARG_* prefix, with suffix letters encoding callback parameters: A for aobj, O for obj, T for type, F for f32, V for pointer, and U for unsigned/integer data.
The new callback argument union is named callbackArg and has fields named by payload kind: f for f32, d for u32 data, and v for void pointer.
The AObj struct and API continue to use HSD_AObj naming, with typedefs and declarations kept in src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a handwritten asm dispatcher into C by matching the old branch-table entries to enum switch cases.
Eliminated jtbl_80405FEC from .data by getting callbackForeachFunc to compile into the required dispatch pattern.
Used no default case in the switch; unsupported arg_type values fall through and return, matching the old assembly's out-of-range path to lbl_803646E4.
Preserved variable argument callback behavior through a generic void (*func)() function pointer rather than introducing multiple typed callback prototypes.
Used lbl_804D5D08 in an assert call instead of __FILE__ as a data/reference matching adjustment.
follow_up_queries: Find other sysdolphin baselib functions with asm jump tables that could be converted to enum switches.
Search for other callbackForeachFunc callers to verify callbackArg usage and argument-format enum conventions.
Search for remaining FObjData pointer usage where a smaller union or callback-specific type may be more accurate.
Find other asserts using __FILE__ that may need a string label such as lbl_* for matching.
